title: Opus 4.6 vs Sonnet 4.6 — Document Analysis Quality & Cost
description: Does Opus 4.6 produce meaningfully better document analysis than Sonnet 4.6 for family archive documents, and is any quality difference worth the additional cost and latency?
key_findings: Opus 4.6 is 25% faster than Sonnet 4.6 (114.7s vs 153.0s for 5 docs)
Opus uses 30% fewer output tokens (5,415 vs 7,721) while extracting more detail
Document type classification is identical across all 5 documents
Opus extracts more dates (20 vs 15 on investment record), more specifics from degraded text
Opus makes better judgment calls (excludes letterhead governor from key_people, flags deed as financial)
Both models covered by Max plan; API-equivalent cost for 116 docs: Opus ~$13, Sonnet ~$3.50
Recommendation: use Opus for remaining documents — quality edge is real, speed is better, cost is covered
